title: Hook extension: archive-hint.cjs second signal (pending overflow → review skill)
description: Single-script approach (per planning agent decision; aligns with rc.2 KT-D entry on disk). Extend the existing archive-hint.cjs decide() function with a second signal: pending overflow. Trigger when (count of .fabric/knowledge/pending/<type>/*.md across all types >= 10) OR (oldest pending file mtime >= 7 days ago). When second signal fires AND archive signal does NOT fire, recommend the fabric-review skill via stdout JSON {decision:'block', reason:..., signal:'review'}. When BOTH fire, archive signal takes precedence (returning to recent work is more urgent than long-tail review). When neither fires, exit silently.

files.target: decide() function + CONSTANTS block + readPendingStats helper
files.change: Add CONSTANTS: PENDING_DIR='knowledge/pending', PENDING_TYPES=['decisions','pitfalls','guidelines','models','processes'], THRESHOLD_PENDING_COUNT=10, THRESHOLD_PENDING_AGE_DAYS=7, MS_PER_DAY=24*60*60*1000. Add helper readPendingStats(projectRoot) returning {count, oldestAgeMs|null} by walking PENDING_DIR/<type>/*.md and stat()-ing each. Extend decide(events, now, pendingStats) with branch: const triggerByPendingCount = pendingStats.count>=THRESHOLD_PENDING_COUNT; const triggerByPendingAge = pendingStats.oldestAgeMs!==null && pendingStats.oldestAgeMs/MS_PER_DAY>=THRESHOLD_PENDING_AGE_DAYS; on archive trigger return {decision,reason,signal:'archive'}; on review-only trigger return {decision,reason,signal:'review'}; precedence: archive > review. main() reads pendingStats before calling decide.

implementation: Read packages/cli/templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs to confirm current structure (decide returns {decision,reason} or null; main wraps in try/catch with silent-fail)

Add CONSTANTS lines after line 14: PENDING_DIR='knowledge/pending', PENDING_TYPES=['decisions','pitfalls','guidelines','models','processes'], THRESHOLD_PENDING_COUNT=10, THRESHOLD_PENDING_AGE_DAYS=7, MS_PER_DAY=24*60*60*1000

Add helper function readPendingStats(projectRoot, now): walks PENDING_DIR for each type via existsSync+readdirSync (sync for hook simplicity); for each .md file run statSync().mtimeMs; track oldest = min(mtimeMs); count = total; return {count, oldestAgeMs: count>0 ? nowMs-oldest : null}. ENOENT → return {count:0, oldestAgeMs:null} (silent like readLedger)

Extend decide(events, now, pendingStats) signature — pendingStats becomes 3rd arg. Compute triggerByPendingCount = pendingStats.count >= THRESHOLD_PENDING_COUNT. Compute triggerByPendingAge = pendingStats.oldestAgeMs !== null && (pendingStats.oldestAgeMs / MS_PER_DAY) >= THRESHOLD_PENDING_AGE_DAYS. Set triggerReview = triggerByPendingCount || triggerByPendingAge

Decision logic: if (triggerByCount || triggerByHours) → archive signal (existing path) → return {decision:'block', reason: <existing message + signal-tag>, signal:'archive'}; else if (triggerReview) → return {decision:'block', reason:`已积累 ${pendingStats.count} 条待审核知识${pendingStats.oldestAgeMs!==null ? `，最早一条距今 ${(pendingStats.oldestAgeMs/MS_PER_DAY).toFixed(1)} 天`:''} — 建议调用 fabric-review skill 审核 pending/ 条目。`, signal:'review'}; else return null
Update main(env, stdio): call readPendingStats(cwd, now) and pass as 3rd arg to decide
Update module.exports.CONSTANTS to include the new threshold constants
Confirm vitest still excludes .cjs from coverage glob (per rc2-coverage-gate.mjs:163 note); coverage measured via in-process test imports
Commit as 'feat(hooks): extend archive-hint with pending-overflow second signal (rc.3 TASK-004)'

reference.pattern: Single hook script with pure decide() function + main() wrapper + module.exports surface for in-process tests
reference.examples: Extend not fork — keep single readLedger + single decide; add second signal as parallel branch in decide; precedence rule documented inline

criteria: decide() returns {signal:'review'} when pendingStats.count >= 10 and no archive trigger
decide() returns {signal:'review'} when pendingStats.oldestAgeMs / MS_PER_DAY >= 7 and no archive trigger
decide() returns {signal:'archive'} when both archive and review triggers fire (precedence)
decide() returns null when no triggers fire (existing behavior preserved)
main() invocation includes pendingStats — verified by integration test stubbing process.cwd()
All existing archive-hint.test.ts tests still pass (regression-free)
New tests achieve >=90% line coverage on the hook script (per in-process test setup)
stdout JSON includes signal field on every block decision (downstream skill router reads it)

rationale.chosen_approach: Single-script extension — adds second signal as parallel branch in decide() with precedence rule
alternatives_considered: Fork to review-hint.cjs as separate Stop hook entry — rejected: duplicates readLedger, requires second template install, requires second hooks.Stop entry merge in skills-and-hooks.ts. KT-D entry on disk argues against this
Drop archive signal entirely and ship only review hint — rejected: archive signal still has value for active sessions (prompts archival mid-session before context fades)
Separate threshold per type (e.g. 10 pitfalls but 5 decisions) — rejected: complexity not justified at rc.3
decision_factors: Single template install (TASK-006 unchanged for hook)
Existing fabric-archive Stop hook entry already merged into .claude/settings.json — adding signal field needs no config change
rationale.tradeoffs: decide() now has 4 branches instead of 2; mitigated by inline precedence comments and test coverage of all 4 outcomes

manual_checks: Run archive-hint.cjs with this very repo as cwd (3 pending entries currently exist, count<10, age<7d so neither review trigger fires) — verify silent exit
Seed 10+ dummy pending files in tmpdir; run hook; verify stdout contains signal:review JSON
success_metrics: archive-hint.test.ts passes 100% with new tests added

risks.description: readdirSync on missing dir throws; statSync on non-existent file throws — must wrap in try/catch to preserve silent-fail invariant
risks.mitigation: Use existsSync guards before readdir/stat; surrounding try/catch in main already silences any escape
risks.fallback: If a non-fatal error escapes try/catch, ledger contains a one-line warning — acceptable degraded mode

risks.description: MS_PER_DAY drift if hook tested on long-running clock — flaky tests around 7-day boundary
risks.mitigation: Tests inject fixed `now` value via main(env,stdio) signature (already supported)
